title: Lemma 2.5: AFP projection identities $\lambda_1(e_B)+\lambda_2(e_B)=1+e_B$ and the key operator inequalities

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location:** Proof of Lemma 2.5 (AFP base lemma), final paragraph.

The contradiction uses, without proof:
1. $\lambda_1(e_B)+\lambda_2(e_B)=1+e_B$, where $\lambda_i(e_B)$ projects onto vectors of $L^2(M_1\ast_B M_2)$ "beginning in $M_{i+1}^o$ or $B$" ([TY25, Prop. 4.16]).
2. $u_1^*\lambda_1(e_B)u_1+u_2^*\lambda_1(e_B)u_2\le\lambda_2(e_B)$ and $u_3^*\lambda_2(e_B)u_3\le\lambda_1(e_B)$.

Identity (1) is a structural fact about the AFP $L^2$-space and needs a reduced-word proof. Inequalities (2) are **exactly where the hypotheses $E_B(u_1)=E_B(u_2)=E_B(u_3)=E_B(u_1^*u_2)=0$ are used**: they guarantee that $u_1\xi,u_2\xi$ are orthogonal and that left multiplication by $u_i$ raises word-length appropriately, mapping $\lambda_2$-vectors into $\lambda_1$-vectors (and vice versa). These must be derived explicitly, verifying each $E_B$-vanishing hypothesis at the point of use.
